title: Command Center Readability and Action Card Redesign
old_issue_summary: Phase 24B rendered command actions as compact inline buttons inside a tight row.
The button title span used truncation, producing unreadable labels such as S..., E..., M..., and S... at the live desktop layout.
The snapshot count occupied a separate flex item beside the action row, reducing available action width.

new_command_card_layout.desktop_behavior: four readable cards where wide enough, otherwise a clean two-by-two grid
new_command_card_layout.narrow_behavior: cards stack in a single column
visual_hierarchy: snapshot status chip in header area

action_copy_used.Search Parcel: Find a parcel and show zoning, flood, school, development, and service context.
action_copy_used.Explore Countywide: Open layer controls and review countywide indicators.
action_copy_used.Model Lab: Explore internal development model research and safe research signals.
action_copy_used.Save Snapshot: Capture the current map and intelligence brief for an executive report.

snapshot_count_placement.reason: prevents saved snapshot count from squeezing the action card grid

mode_behavior_verification.Search Parcel: Activates parcel mode and focuses the top search input.
mode_behavior_verification.Explore Countywide: Activates countywide mode and opens layer controls.
mode_behavior_verification.Model Lab: Activates Model Lab mode and shows Model Lab Intelligence.
mode_behavior_verification.Save Snapshot: Dispatches snapshot capture and switches to Snapshot Capture mode.

dev_stack.frontend: http://localhost:3000 responded with 200 and was owned by the CFS Next dev process
dev_stack.backend: http://127.0.0.1:8000 was owned by the CFS Uvicorn process

browser_behavior.Explore Countywide: passed; countywide intelligence and map layers were visible
browser_behavior.Model Lab: passed; Model Lab Intelligence was visible and overlay remained off by default
browser_behavior.Save Snapshot: passed; Snapshot Capture state became visible

remaining_known_issues: In-app browser screenshot capture timed out on the ArcGIS-heavy scene during QA, so browser smoke used DOM/layout checks instead of a screenshot artifact.
Snapshot saved confirmation can be transient while map capture completes; the smoke test verified Snapshot Capture mode rather than relying on the toast timing.
